- **`open spec fn bisect_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:2542](./src/interval.rs#L2542)

- **`open spec fn split_at_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:2551](./src/interval.rs#L2551)
  > Split at an arbitrary rational point p in [lo, hi].
- **`open spec fn subdivide_point_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:2560](./src/interval.rs#L2560)
  > N-way uniform subdivision: n equal-width pieces. Piece k = [lo + k*(hi-lo)/n, lo + (k+1)*(hi-lo)/n].
- **`open spec fn subdivide_piece_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:2570](./src/interval.rs#L2570)
  > The k-th piece of an n-way uniform subdivision.
- **`open spec fn sign_change_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:2909](./src/interval.rs#L2909)
  > Whether two values have opposite signs (one positive, one negative).
- **`open spec fn scalar_newton_step_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:2918](./src/interval.rs#L2918)
  > N(X) = x_mid - f(x_mid)/f'(X) ‚à© X. Returns None if f'(X) contains zero (division undefined).
- **`open spec fn horner_eval_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:2940](./src/interval.rs#L2940)
  > Evaluate polynomial over an interval using Horner's method. coeffs = [c‚ÇÄ, c‚ÇÅ, ..., c‚Çô], result encloses all p(x) for x ‚àà X. p(x) = c‚ÇÄ + c‚ÇÅx + c‚ÇÇx¬≤ + ... = c‚ÇÄ + x*(c‚ÇÅ + x*(c‚ÇÇ + ...))
- **`open spec fn poly_derivative_coeffs_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:3018](./src/interval.rs#L3018)
  > Compute derivative polynomial coefficients. If p(x) = c‚ÇÄ + c‚ÇÅx + c‚ÇÇx¬≤ + ..., then p'(x) = c‚ÇÅ + 2c‚ÇÇx + 3c‚ÇÉx¬≤ + ... Output: [c‚ÇÅ, 2*c‚ÇÇ, 3*c‚ÇÉ, ...]
- **`open spec fn hausdorff_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:3034](./src/interval.rs#L3034)
  > Hausdorff distance between two intervals. max(|lo‚ÇÅ - lo‚ÇÇ|, |hi‚ÇÅ - hi‚ÇÇ|)
- **`open spec fn gap_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:3344](./src/interval.rs#L3344)
  > Gap (separation) between two intervals. Positive iff the intervals are disjoint.

## `interval::Interval`

### Spec Functions

- **`open spec fn wf_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:17](./src/interval.rs#L17)
  > Well-formedness: the interval is non-empty (lo <= hi).
- **`open spec fn contains_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:22](./src/interval.rs#L22)
  > Point containment: lo <= x <= hi.
- **`open spec fn contains_interval_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:27](./src/interval.rs#L27)
  > Sub-interval containment: self contains other entirely.
- **`open spec fn width_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:32](./src/interval.rs#L32)
  > Interval width: hi - lo.
- **`open spec fn midpoint_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:37](./src/interval.rs#L37)
  > Interval midpoint: (lo + hi) / 2.
- **`open spec fn is_point_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:42](./src/interval.rs#L42)
  > Degenerate (point) interval: lo and hi represent the same value.
- **`open spec fn from_point_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:47](./src/interval.rs#L47)
  > Construct a point interval [x, x] (spec-level).
- **`open spec fn overlap_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:52](./src/interval.rs#L52)
  > Two intervals overlap (share at least one point).
- **`open spec fn hull_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:57](./src/interval.rs#L57)
  > Convex hull: smallest interval containing both self and other.
- **`open spec fn intersect_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:66](./src/interval.rs#L66)
  > Intersection: largest interval contained in both self and other. Returns None if the intervals don't overlap.
- **`open spec fn certainly_positive_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:79](./src/interval.rs#L79)
  > The interval is entirely positive: lo > 0.
- **`open spec fn certainly_negative_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:84](./src/interval.rs#L84)
  > The interval is entirely negative: hi < 0.
- **`open spec fn certainly_zero_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:89](./src/interval.rs#L89)
  > The interval is certainly zero: degenerate point at 0.
- **`open spec fn certainly_nonneg_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:94](./src/interval.rs#L94)
  > The interval is entirely nonneg: lo >= 0.
- **`open spec fn certainly_nonpos_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:99](./src/interval.rs#L99)
  > The interval is entirely nonpos: hi <= 0.
- **`open spec fn possibly_zero_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:104](./src/interval.rs#L104)
  > The interval possibly contains zero: lo <= 0 <= hi.
- **`open spec fn sign_definite_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:111](./src/interval.rs#L111)
  > Sign determination: Some(1) if positive, Some(-1) if negative, Some(0) if certainly zero, None if indeterminate.
- **`open spec fn certainly_lt_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:124](./src/interval.rs#L124)
  > self is certainly less than rhs: self.hi < rhs.lo.
- **`open spec fn certainly_le_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:129](./src/interval.rs#L129)
  > self is certainly <= rhs: self.hi <= rhs.lo.
- **`open spec fn certainly_equal_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:134](./src/interval.rs#L134)
  > Both intervals are the same point.
- **`open spec fn possibly_lt_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:139](./src/interval.rs#L139)
  > It's possible that some x in self is less than some y in rhs.
- **`open spec fn disjoint_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:144](./src/interval.rs#L144)
  > The intervals are disjoint: self.hi < rhs.lo or rhs.hi < self.lo.
- **`open spec fn tighten_nonneg_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:151](./src/interval.rs#L151)
  > Tighten to nonneg: clamp lo to max(0, lo).
- **`open spec fn tighten_nonpos_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:159](./src/interval.rs#L159)
  > Tighten to nonpos: clamp hi to min(0, hi).
- **`open spec fn tighten_positive_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:167](./src/interval.rs#L167)
  > Tighten to positive: clamp lo to max(0, lo), requires hi > 0.
- **`open spec fn square_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:177](./src/interval.rs#L177)
  > Squaring: tighter than mul(a, a) because it exploits x¬≤ ‚â• 0.
- **`open spec fn pow_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:202](./src/interval.rs#L202)
  > Integer power: [a,b]^n (naive recursive via mul for simplicity).
- **`open spec fn fma_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:213](./src/interval.rs#L213)
  > Fused multiply-add: a*b + c (no intermediate interval rounding).
- **`open spec fn add_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:220](./src/interval.rs#L220)
  > Addition: [a,b] + [c,d] = [a+c, b+d].
- **`open spec fn neg_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:228](./src/interval.rs#L228)
  > Negation: -[a,b] = [-b, -a].
- **`open spec fn sub_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:236](./src/interval.rs#L236)
  > Subtraction: [a,b] - [c,d] = [a-d, b-c].
- **`open spec fn mul_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:244](./src/interval.rs#L244)
  > Multiplication: [a,b] * [c,d] = [min(ac,ad,bc,bd), max(ac,ad,bc,bd)].
- **`open spec fn scale_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:256](./src/interval.rs#L256)
  > Scalar multiplication: k * [a,b] = [min(k*a, k*b), max(k*a, k*b)].
- **`open spec fn abs_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:266](./src/interval.rs#L266)
  > Absolute value: |[a,b]|.
- **`open spec fn recip_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:281](./src/interval.rs#L281)
  > Reciprocal: 1/[a,b] = [1/b, 1/a] (defined when 0 not in [a,b]).
- **`open spec fn div_spec`** ‚Äî [interval.rs:289](./src/interval.rs#L289)
  > Division: [a,b] / [c,d] = [a,b] * (1/[c,d]) (defined when 0 not in [c,d]).

## `runtime_interval`

### Spec Functions

- **`open spec fn wf_spec`** ‚Äî [runtime_interval.rs:33](./src/runtime_interval.rs#L33)
  > Well-formedness invariant tying runtime witnesses to the ghost model.

### Exec Functions

- **`exec fn from_endpoints`** ‚Äî [runtime_interval.rs:43](./src/runtime_interval.rs#L43)
  > Construct an interval from ordered endpoints [lo, hi]. Takes ownership of both RuntimeRationals.
- **`exec fn from_point`** ‚Äî [runtime_interval.rs:64](./src/runtime_interval.rs#L64)
  > Construct a point interval [x, x] from a single rational.
- **`exec fn add`** ‚Äî [runtime_interval.rs:93](./src/runtime_interval.rs#L93)
  > Addition: [a,b] + [c,d] = [a+c, b+d].
- **`exec fn neg`** ‚Äî [runtime_interval.rs:116](./src/runtime_interval.rs#L116)
  > Negation: -[a,b] = [-b, -a].
- **`exec fn sub`** ‚Äî [runtime_interval.rs:138](./src/runtime_interval.rs#L138)
  > Subtraction: [a,b] - [c,d] = [a-d, b-c].
- **`exec fn mul`** ‚Äî [runtime_interval.rs:161](./src/runtime_interval.rs#L161)
  > Multiplication: [a,b] * [c,d] = [min(ac,ad,bc,bd), max(ac,ad,bc,bd)].
- **`exec fn scale`** ‚Äî [runtime_interval.rs:197](./src/runtime_interval.rs#L197)
  > Scalar multiplication: k * [a,b].
- **`exec fn abs`** ‚Äî [runtime_interval.rs:222](./src/runtime_interval.rs#L222)
  > Absolute value: |[a,b]|.
- **`exec fn recip`** ‚Äî [runtime_interval.rs:280](./src/runtime_interval.rs#L280)
  > Reciprocal: 1/[a,b] = [1/b, 1/a]. Requires 0 not in the interval (entirely positive or entirely negative).
- **`exec fn div`** ‚Äî [runtime_interval.rs:352](./src/runtime_interval.rs#L352)
  > Division: [a,b] / [c,d] = [a,b] * (1/[c,d]). Requires 0 not in the divisor interval.
- **`exec fn intersect`** ‚Äî [runtime_interval.rs:374](./src/runtime_interval.rs#L374)
  > Intersection of two intervals. Returns None if they don't overlap, Some(result) otherwise.
- **`exec fn certainly_positive`** ‚Äî [runtime_interval.rs:406](./src/runtime_interval.rs#L406)
  > Is the interval certainly positive? (lo > 0)
- **`exec fn certainly_negative`** ‚Äî [runtime_interval.rs:415](./src/runtime_interval.rs#L415)
  > Is the interval certainly negative? (hi < 0)
- **`exec fn certainly_zero`** ‚Äî [runtime_interval.rs:424](./src/runtime_interval.rs#L424)
  > Is the interval certainly zero? (point at 0)
- **`exec fn certainly_nonneg`** ‚Äî [runtime_interval.rs:433](./src/runtime_interval.rs#L433)
  > Is the interval certainly nonneg? (lo >= 0)
- **`exec fn certainly_nonpos`** ‚Äî [runtime_interval.rs:442](./src/runtime_interval.rs#L442)
  > Is the interval certainly nonpos? (hi <= 0)
- **`exec fn possibly_zero`** ‚Äî [runtime_interval.rs:451](./src/runtime_interval.rs#L451)
  > Does the interval possibly contain zero? (lo <= 0 <= hi)
- **`exec fn sign_definite`** ‚Äî [runtime_interval.rs:460](./src/runtime_interval.rs#L460)
  > Sign determination: 1 if positive, -1 if negative, 0 if zero, None if indeterminate.
- **`exec fn certainly_lt`** ‚Äî [runtime_interval.rs:476](./src/runtime_interval.rs#L476)
  > Is self certainly less than rhs? (self.hi < rhs.lo)
- **`exec fn certainly_le`** ‚Äî [runtime_interval.rs:484](./src/runtime_interval.rs#L484)
  > Is self certainly <= rhs? (self.hi <= rhs.lo)
- **`exec fn certainly_equal`** ‚Äî [runtime_interval.rs:492](./src/runtime_interval.rs#L492)
  > Are both intervals the same point?
- **`exec fn possibly_lt`** ‚Äî [runtime_interval.rs:500](./src/runtime_interval.rs#L500)
  > Is it possible that some x in self < some y in rhs? (self.lo < rhs.hi)
- **`exec fn disjoint`** ‚Äî [runtime_interval.rs:508](./src/runtime_interval.rs#L508)
  > Are the intervals disjoint?
- **`exec fn tighten_nonneg`** ‚Äî [runtime_interval.rs:516](./src/runtime_interval.rs#L516)
  > Tighten to nonneg: clamp lo to max(0, lo).
- **`exec fn tighten_nonpos`** ‚Äî [runtime_interval.rs:547](./src/runtime_interval.rs#L547)
  > Tighten to nonpos: clamp hi to min(0, hi).
- **`exec fn square`** ‚Äî [runtime_interval.rs:577](./src/runtime_interval.rs#L577)
  > Squaring: tighter than mul(self, self).
- **`exec fn pow`** ‚Äî [runtime_interval.rs:631](./src/runtime_interval.rs#L631)
  > Integer power (naive recursive).
- **`exec fn fma`** ‚Äî [runtime_interval.rs:653](./src/runtime_interval.rs#L653)
  > Fused multiply-add: self * mul_rhs + add_rhs.
- **`exec fn bisect`** ‚Äî [runtime_interval.rs:668](./src/runtime_interval.rs#L668)
  > Bisect at midpoint: returns ([lo, mid], [mid, hi]).
- **`exec fn split_at`** ‚Äî [runtime_interval.rs:713](./src/runtime_interval.rs#L713)
  > Split at an arbitrary rational point p where lo ‚â§ p ‚â§ hi.
- **`exec fn has_sign_change`** ‚Äî [runtime_interval.rs:764](./src/runtime_interval.rs#L764)
  > Check if two function values indicate a sign change.
- **`exec fn scalar_newton_step`** ‚Äî [runtime_interval.rs:778](./src/runtime_interval.rs#L778)
  > Scalar interval Newton step: N(X) = x_mid - f(x_mid)/f'(X) ‚à© X. Returns None if f'(X) contains zero or intersection is empty.
